title: buxus flaviramea
intro: Buxus flaviramea, also known as boxwood, is a type of evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the family Buxaceae. The leaves of this plant contain a toxic compound called buxine, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Buxus flaviramea include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce pain, and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Buxus flaviramea plant are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
